zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Booth於大約1280年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，他的父親Thomas de Booth26歲，母親Agnes de Barton de Notton40歲。他於1320年之前與Agnes de Barton de Notton在英國藍卡爾巴頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1370年在他的家鄉享年92歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Booth于大约1280年出生在英国林可爾史爾时，他的父亲Thomas de Booth26岁，母亲Agnes de Barton de Notton40岁。他于1320年之前与Agnes de Barton de Notton在英国藍卡爾巴頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1370年在他的家乡享年92岁。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Booth was born about 1280, in Lincolnshire, England, his father, Thomas de Booth, was 26 and his mother, Agnes de Barton de Notton, was 40. He married Agnes de Barton de Notton before 1320, in Barton, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died about 1370, in his hometown, at the age of 92.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Booth(이)가 대략 1280년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Booth(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Barton de Notton(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1320년 이전 영국 랭카실르 바턴에서 Agnes de Barton de Notton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1370년 그의 고향에서 92세의 나이로 사망했다.
